meaning: n.耙子,斜度,向船尾的倾斜,放荡者v.搜索,掠过,用耙子耙,(使)倾斜
examples: I watched the men rake leaves into heaps.
Rake up the trash on the ground.
There's a little spade, a rake, a fork and hoe.
Mr Clegg responded that he was not going to "rake over the results of the summit".
Dreary stadiums they don't own and stalling businesses eager to rake in more cash.
